title: "Error: committing container for step" message exposes all env vars
body:
In a docker build with Buildah we ran into the situation where there was not enough disk space available. The process terminates with the following messages:
```
level=error msg="While applying layer: ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: open /src/.cache/pypoetry/...: no space left on device exit status 1"
Error: committing container for step {Env:.....} 
```

The last line also prints out all environment vars which may potentially contain confidential information that should not be written to a log file. Can this printing of env vars be disabled/hidden somehow?

comments.body: I advise against putting sensitive information in environment variables, as they are baked into built images.

> I advise against putting sensitive information in environment variables, as they are baked into built images.

That's certainly true and not in question. I'm still wondering if there's a way to suppress the output in the logs because we cannot protect the developers from doing silly things in their Dockerfiles.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
